text:
Market today: Mild risk-on tone - broad indices holding, millionaire-minting headline confirms 2025 gains are real. No macro bomb today, but Russia/Ukraine escalation and Tesla's 6% drop on good delivery news signals fragile sentiment in growth/momentum names - exactly your space.

Your holdings: CRWD is the fire alarm - down 72.6% and your scanner entry ($192.62) is near current price, but you're already deep underwater; margin makes this uniquely dangerous. SOXL is within $4 of its $200 stop at $196.36, down 22.8% - leveraged decay is grinding you daily. SPAL is down 11.8% and hugging its $21.38 stop at $22.18 - another leveraged name bleeding quietly.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds above $194.58 (scanner support) and $200 (your stop) - a break is a forced decision. Also watch if Tesla's drop bleeds into semis/QQQ tomorrow.

Bottom line: Stop defense on SOXL and SPAL matters more today than any new buy.
plays: SOXL: Your stop is $200, price is $196.36 - already through it. Decide now: cut or accept the scanner's tighter stop at $194.58 as your new line.
CRWD: Down 72.6% on margin is brutal. Scanner shows entry $192.43-$192.62, stop $189.32 - if you're holding, that stop is non-negotiable; if you're uncertain, trim now.
No new buys today - SPAL is bleeding into its stop, two leveraged names need triage first; adding exposure here is the wrong order of operations.
